prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on an outdoor street or storefront scene. A dark, open-top jeep-like vehicle is parked in front of a building with signage above the entrance, though the wording on the sign is not clearly readable. A person is seated in the vehicle.

The view then shifts to a closer, partially reflective or superimposed shot inside or through the vehicle. A person is visible near the dashboard area, and large on-screen product text appears: “MIDLAND CB” with “CONVOY BUDDY” beneath it. The image has a layered, dissolving effect, making some details hard to distinguish.

Next, the commercial continues with another close-up view through or around the vehicle, again using overlapping imagery. The scene appears outdoors, with trees or foliage visible. A vertical object that may be an antenna or vehicle frame element is prominent in the foreground.

The following shot shows a smiling person wearing a light-colored helmet or hat. The person is seen close-up and appears to be outdoors, with another overlaid image of trees or branches across the frame. One arm is raised or extended upward/rightward, though the exact action is unclear because of the superimposition.

The final product shot shows the open-top vehicle on a small bridge or raised wooden crossing in a wooded or brushy setting. Large bold text fills the lower portion of the screen: “MIDLAND CB” and “CONVOY BUDDY.” A smaller line of text appears underneath but is too blurry to read clearly.

The commercial then cuts to a black frame.
